conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_003. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
I need to return items from two different orders — a shirt from ORD-6042 and a book from ORD-6043.

I can help with both. I found the shirt in ORD-6042 and the book in ORD-6043, and both orders are within their return windows right now.

To start the returns, I just need the reason for each item. For example: changed mind, defective, wrong item, not as described, damaged in transit, or missing.

rules.agent_computes_amount: process_return requires the agent to submit the net refund as `amount` on confirm. Compute it from the preview's component breakdown: base_item_price (after promo redistribution) minus restocking_fee plus restocking_discount minus shipping_clawback minus bulk_clawback minus repeat_surcharge minus paid_return_shipping_fee. The env writes the submitted amount verbatim — skipping a component (e.g. forgetting the Gold restocking discount) produces a wrong refund_amount and fails state scoring.

window_extensions.prime: Prime shipping members get +15 additional days
window_extensions.seasonal: Orders placed in November or December extend through January 31 of the following year. Seasonal extension applies if it gives more time than tier/prime extensions.
window_extensions.store_credit_grace: Up to 15 days past the return window: store credit only.

eligibility.defective_items: No window restriction for defective, wrong item, or damaged in transit.

restocking_fee.base: 15% restocking fee for opened electronics returned for changed_mind.
restocking_fee.auto_applied: Restocking fee and any tier discount are folded into the process_return refund amount automatically; the result reports the discount under 'restocking_discount' for transparency.

return_shipping_fee.low_value: Orders with subtotal < $50: customer pays $8 return shipping fee.
return_shipping_fee.fault_exempt: Defective, wrong item, damaged in transit, or missing: always free return shipping.
return_shipping_fee.deduction: Return shipping fee, when charged, is deducted from the return refund.

bulk_purchase_clawback.applies_when: Order originally qualified for a bulk discount (3+ items with a discount code) AND the return drops remaining count below 3.
bulk_purchase_clawback.amount: $5 per remaining item, deducted from the return refund.
bulk_purchase_clawback.no_discount_code: If order had no discount code, no clawback applies.

free_shipping_clawback.applies_when: Order originally qualified for free shipping (subtotal >= $100) AND the return drops remaining subtotal below $100.
free_shipping_clawback.amount: $8 standard shipping fee is deducted from the return refund. This is a flat policy charge, not the original shipping cost (which was $0 on free-shipping orders).
free_shipping_clawback.fault_exempt: Defective, wrong item, damaged in transit, or missing: no clawback (customer-fault returns only).
free_shipping_clawback.paid_shipping: If the order did not qualify for free shipping originally (subtotal < $100), no clawback applies.

repeat_category_surcharge.rule: Returning 2+ items from the same product category in one order: $5 surcharge per additional return (first return in each category is free).
repeat_category_surcharge.different_categories: Returns from different categories do not trigger the surcharge.
repeat_category_surcharge.deduction: Surcharge is deducted from the return refund.

conversation.content: Sure — the shirt from ORD-6042 was just a changed mind, and the book from ORD-6043 arrived damaged.

Both returns are eligible.

- Shirt from ORD-6042: refund will be $59
- Book from ORD-6043: refund will be $24

If you want, I can submit both returns now.

Thanks — before you submit, can you show a quick preview that keeps them separate by order?

I want to confirm it’s ORD-6042 = shirt (changed mind) and ORD-6043 = book (damaged) before you finalize both.

Absolutely:

- ORD-6042 — Premium Cotton Shirt — reason: changed mind — estimated refund: $59
- ORD-6043 — The Great Algorithm (Hardcover) — reason: damaged in transit — estimated refund: $24

If that looks right, reply “confirm” and I’ll finalize both returns.
Confirm — that preview looks right, and thanks for keeping ORD-6042/shirt and ORD-6043/book separate. Please finalize both returns.

Done — both returns are finalized.

- ORD-6042 shirt: $59 refund to your original payment method
- ORD-6043 book: $24 refund to your original payment method

A return label was issued for the damaged book. The shirt return did not generate a label.
Perfect, thanks for finalizing both and keeping them separate. [TASK_DONE]
